mission.title: Install and Configure Grpc on Kubernetes
mission.description: gRPC is a modern, open-source high-performance remote procedure call (RPC) framework that enables client and server applications to communicate transparently. Installing gRPC allows you to build connected systems efficiently across various programming languages.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the Helm repository for gRPC applications with the following command:
```bash
helm repo add grpc-app https://sindvero.github.io/distributed-grpc-app
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install the gRPC Helm chart
steps.description:
Install the gRPC application using the Helm chart:
```bash
helm install grpc-app grpc-app/grpc-app --namespace grpc --create-namespace --version 0.1.0
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the gRPC pods are running:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace grpc
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Configure resource limits for the gRPC application:
```bash
kubectl set resources deployment/grpc-app --namespace grpc --limits=cpu=500m,memory=512Mi
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the gRPC application release:
```bash
helm uninstall grpc-app --namespace grpc
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any custom resource definitions (CRDs) created by the gRPC application:
```bash
kubectl delete crd grpc-apps.grpc.sindvero.io
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the namespace used for the gRPC application:
```bash
kubectl delete namespace grpc
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, back up the current configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace grpc -o yaml > grpc-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to get the latest charts:
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the gRPC Helm chart
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the gRPC application to the latest version:
```bash
helm upgrade grpc-app grpc-app/grpc-app --namespace grpc --version 0.1.0
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the gRPC pods are running after the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace grpc
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
If pods are in a CrashLoopBackOff state, check the logs for errors:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace grpc
``` 
Common issues may include misconfiguration or resource limits being too low.

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If the service is not reachable, ensure the service is running:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace grpc
``` 
Also, check if the correct ports are exposed.

troubleshooting.title: High latency in RPC calls
troubleshooting.description:
If you experience high latency, check the resource usage of the pods:
```bash
kubectl top pods --namespace grpc
``` 
Consider increasing resource limits if usage is high.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of gRPC will have the application running in the specified namespace with all pods in a 'Running' state. You can verify the installation by checking the status of the pods and services.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add grpc-app https://sindvero.github.io/distributed-grpc-app
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have Kubernetes cluster access and Helm installed on your local machine.
